title: stachys recta subsp. psammophila
intro: Stachys recta subsp. psammophila, commonly known as the sand-wort or psammophila betony, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other secondary metabolites. The health benefits of Stachys recta subsp. psammophila include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant protection. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be found in dried or fresh form in herbal supplements and remedies.
